sourceMovie.title: Juice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Juice (1992) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ernest R. Dickerson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Natural Born Killers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Juice clicked for you, Natural Born Killers (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Juice?
faq.answer: Try Natural Born Killers, Layer Cake, Gloria—each shares DNA with Juice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Juice?
faq.answer: Juice (1992) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Juice worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
